content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

April 22, 2004

Charlene Ayers
10801 Dewitt Court
El Cajon, CA 92020

Re:    Your Request for Advice
       Our File No. W-04-090

Dear Ms. Ayers:

        This letter is in response to your request for written advice on the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹ Under California Code of Regulations
18329(b)(8)(B), copy enclosed, unless you are an authorized representative of Bruce
Tabb, a developer in your area for whom you seek assistance, the Fair Political Practices
Commission cannot address your questions or provide analysis. However, if you believe
a violation of the Political Reform Act has occurred, you have the option of filing a
complaint with our Enforcement Division. I have enclosed a fact sheet explaining the
complaint process procedure.

        Your request for a copy of Sansone Advice Letter No. I-03-058 has been processed as a
Public Records Act request and will forwarded under separate cover. In addition, your letter
will be placed in the rulemaking file as a comment on the proposed regulations and you will be
added to the interested persons mailing list. Please note, the project about which you inquire
will be discussed by the Commission at the May 13, 2004 Commission meeting. The agenda
will be available on the Commission's website at www.fppc.ca.gov.

        If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact Natalie
Bocanegra at (916) 322-5660.

                                                    Sincerely,

                                                    Luisa Menchaca
                                                    General Counsel

                                                    By:    John W. Wallace
                                                           Assistant General Counsel
Enclosures
JWW:jg
I:\AdviceLtrs\04-090

¹. Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.conclusion: Under California Code of Regulations 18329(b)(8)(B), the FPPC cannot address the requester's questions or provide analysis unless the requester is an authorized representative of the developer in question; however, the requester may file a complaint with the Enforcement Division if a violation is believed to have occurred.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester sought written advice on conflict-of-interest provisions under the Political Reform Act, and specifically requested a copy of a prior FPPC advice letter regarding a local development project.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the requester does not appear to be an authorized representative of the developer, but directed the requester to the complaint process if violations are suspected.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks traditional QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers. The legal question and response are embedded in the body of the letter. This is a declination/limitation letter, not a full advice letter. The document references regulations and prior advice letter No. I-03-058.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requester sought written advice on conflict-of-interest provisions under the Political Reform Act, and specifically requested a copy of a prior FPPC advice letter regarding a local development project.

CONCLUSION: Under California Code of Regulations 18329(b)(8)(B), the FPPC cannot address the requester's questions or provide analysis unless the requester is an authorized representative of the developer in question; however, the requester may file a complaint with the Enforcement Division if a violation is believed to have occurred.

embedding.summary: This is a declination letter from the FPPC responding to Charlene Ayers' request for written advice regarding conflict-of-interest issues related to a local developer. The FPPC declined to provide analysis due to lack of authorization, but offered the alternative of filing a complaint and processed her request for a prior advice letter as a Public Records Act request.
